A Measuring Rod for Growth

Blanche B. Stoddard
Member, Relief Society General Board

[Address delivered at the annual general Relief Society conference in the Tabernacle, Thursday afternoon, September 30, 1948.]

THIS morning we had a wonderful demonstration of the growth of our building fund which will make possible the realization of a great Relief Society dream. For a moment this afternoon, I would like to speak of a measuring rod for our own individual growth, the following of which may make it possible for us to realize some of our dreams.

We have a beautiful song which says: "I know that my Redeemer lives, and because he lives, I, too, shall live again." The sacrifice of Jesus has ensured the resurrection. We shall literally live again. But his coming has brought to us another kind of living, a rich, full, abundant living that we do not have to wait to enjoy in some hereafter. Before the time of Jesus the old Mosaic Law was in effect, "An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth," but Jesus brought us the greater law of love, of tolerance, and forgiveness. He gave us the key for this rich, abundant life, and his gospel is highly practical. It is something that we can put into our daily lives and really live, and
notes: Found the beginning of Blanche B. Stoddard's article 'A Measuring Rod for Growth' at the end of the Misc file. The text flows naturally into the Art 1 file which starts mid-sentence. The Art 1 filename appears to be incorrectly labeled as 'Carrot Marmalade' when it should be this article.
